title: Photolithography for microfluidics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2sfj2","text":"Photolithography for microfluidic mold fabrication, successfully tested at the Scottish Microelectronics Centre. Produces rounded flow layer using positive resist SPR 220-7 with ~12um features, and rectangular control layer using negative resist SU-8-3035 with ~35um features). steps: HMDS priming
O2 plasma treatment
Prebake
Spin coat with SPR 220-7 (Megaposit) SPR_220_DATA_SHEET_RH.pdf
Softbake
Exposure
Post-exposure bake
Develop with MF26A
Reflow
Prime a clean Si wafer in a sealed box with HMDS for 00:10:00
Transfer to hotplate and carry out pre-bake at 120 °C for 00:12:00 .
Transfer wafer to spin coater and run following programme using SPR 220-7 resist: 1. 400 rpm, °C, 30 seconds 2. 1000 rpm, °C, 1 minute 40 seconds at 200 rpm/s. This coats the wafer with ~12 um.
Transfer to hotplate and carry out soft-bake at 105 °C for 00:06:00 . Remove promptly when done.

Expose with the following settings (for a Karl Suss MA8 mask aligner): Multiple exposure steps: [ 00:01:20 exposure + 00:00:10 wait] x 2 cycles, for a total of 160s (=638mJ/cm^2) WEC=cont Expose type = prox 40um alignment gap 5um expose gap WEC offset OFF N2 purge NO The Karl Suss machine has a flux of 3.99 mW/cm^2 as measured from last maintenance. We would like an exposure of 660 mJ/cm^2.
Wait: leave the wafer in a sealed wafer box for 02:00:00 .
Transfer to a hotplate and hold at 110 °C for 00:05:00
Wait: leave the wafer in a sealed wafer box for 00:45:00
Develop right side up for up to 00:05:00 , rinse with DI water, and dry gently with compressed N2.

Place the wafer on a hotplate at room temperature, then ramp up to 170 °C (at a ramp rate of ~0.5*C/s). Hold at 170 °C for 00:40:00 , then turn off hotplate and allow to ramp down to room temperature (takes a few hours). Before reflow After reflow
Spin coat with SU-8-3035 (Kayaku) KAM-SU-8-3000-Datasheet-7.10-final.pdf
Softbake
Exposure
Post-exposure bake
Wait for a minimum of 00:10:00 .
Develop
Hardbake
Treat with O2 plasma for 10 minutes in barrel asher. (At SMC, Electrotech 508 with typical parameters: forward power 350W, flow 32%, pressure 0.8 torr)

Spin coat with SU8-3035 using the following programme with ramp rate of 100rpm/s for all steps: 1. 500 rpm, °C, 10 seconds 2. 3000 rpm, °C, 1 minute 5 seconds = 25s ramp + 40s hold 3. 4000 rpm, °C, 1 second 3. 3000 rpm, °C, 5 seconds 3. 0 rpm, °C, 30 seconds Make sure the spin coater has a plastic liner installed. If necessary clean back and edge of wafer with wipe soaked in PGMEA.
Place on hotplate for 00:12:30 at 95 °C Let wafer come to room temp (few mins) before exposure. If there are wrinkles, place on hotplate until wrinkles disappear.

Expose with the following settings (for a Karl Suss MA8 mask aligner): Multiple exposure steps: [ 00:00:18 exposure + 00:00:10 wait] x 5 cycles, for a total of 90s (=359mJ/cm^2) WEC=cont Expose type = soft 30um alignment gap 5um expose gap WEC offset OFF N2 purge NO
a. Place on hotplate at 65 °C for 00:01:00 . b. Move to second hotplate and hold at 95 °C for 00:04:00 .

Develop upside down in PGMEA for 00:02:00 , and exchange with new PGMEA for a further 00:02:00 . Check for underdevelopment each time on the microscope (white film). If necessary continue development for up to 6 min. Underdevelopment after 2+1 min Correct development after 2+2 min
Rinse with IPA and dry gently with compressed air.
Ramp to 135 °C , and hold at 135 °C for 02:00:00 .

Silanization must be carried out before first use, to prevent PDMS sticking to wafers. Leave 500 µL of chlorotrimethylsilane (Sigma 386529) in an upturned Falcon cap, inside a sealed box with the wafers until the solvent has completely evaporated.
creator: Nadanai Laohakunakorn
